# skill-builder — cross-agent SKILL.md workflows by Ming

> Practical Agent Skills used with Claude Code, Codex, and compatible agents. Public
> repository: https://github.com/m1nga/skill-builder

## Skills

- map-product-system: map a product end to end across user journeys, capabilities, mandates, data, agents/services/humans, architecture, failures, governance, gaps, and delivery slices
- loop-system-architect: design, audit, and operationalize agent loops with machine-readable contracts, triggers, persistent state, independent verification, failure recovery, open skill discovery, experience distillation, controlled evolution, and a deterministic linter
- iteration-close: close a product iteration in any repo — distill decisions into the repo, delete dead files (git history is the archive), verify upload and a cold-start takeover probe, rehearse the one-command bootstrap, tag and seed the next iteration
- experience-pack: keep a per-project experience ledger, distill it into portable lessons via an experience-vs-decision boundary test (experience inherits, decisions die), and reuse a packaged five-rebuild contamination saga with five prevention advices
- product-experience-officer: perform a zero-context first-time-user product audit and return prioritized, actionable UX findings
- morning-brief: create an overnight cross-session work digest with progress, open loops, and evidence-based work improvements
- explain-ai-reply: explain a confusing AI response against the original request and separate verified, claimed, proposed, failed, missed, and user-required work
- think4ming: thinking-partner protocol — restore the real need, inventory live reality, open options, one recommendation, hand back the decisions
- prompt4ming: lightly clarify rough or voice-dictated input while preserving meaning and avoiding prompt overengineering
- prompt-craft: turn casual ideas into AI-ready prompts with an optional bring-your-own local knowledge layer
- coffee-brewing: interpret coffee photos and taste feedback to recommend recipes and iterative dial-in adjustments

## Recommended pairing

Use map-product-system directly on raw product thinking. Use prompt4ming first only when a
portable, lightly cleaned prompt is needed; chaining is optional.

## Install

- All skills: `npx skills add m1nga/skill-builder`
- One skill: `npx skills add https://github.com/m1nga/skill-builder --skill map-product-system`
- Manual Claude Code location: `~/.claude/skills/`
- Manual Codex location: `~/.codex/skills/`

Self-authored skills are published automatically when Codex or Claude finishes and validates
them. Private knowledge, personal business data, and third-party skills are excluded.
